016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.DeserializationFeature;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JavaType;
020import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode;
021import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;
022import org.slf4j.Logger;
023import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
024
025import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
026import java.lang.reflect.Type;
027import java.time.Duration;
028import java.net.URI;
029import java.util.Collection;
030import java.util.Objects;
031import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
032import java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException;
033import java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException;
034import java.util.function.Function;
035import java.util.function.Supplier;
036
037// We're overusing inheritance in this hierarchy. We are breaking Effective Java, Item 18: Favor composition over inheritance.
038// We're using inheritance to configure:
039// A. The set of JSON-RPC methods that are supported, e.g. `getblockcount()` (which creates potential conflicts with `send()` etc)
040// B. The JSON mapping implementation. Which for now and the foreseeable future is Jackson only.
041//
042// TODO: Maybe look at extracting a class with some common "mapper" functions between the two transport implementations
043//
044// The proper separation for (A) is probably a complete separation. There should be no required inheritance to implement
045// a client with a set of methods. Internally the client would have a transport and a mapper and those could optionally be made available
046// via some accessor methods if the client application deems necessary.
047// At the heart of (B) (at least as currently implemented) is the mapping from a Java (or Groovy) method name to (i) a JSON-RPC method
048// name, (ii) optional parameter type-conversion for JSON serialization in the request, and (iii) type mapping for the deserialization
049// of the `result` field in the JsonRpcResponse<RSLT>. It might be helpful to think of this as two functional mappings:
050// (1) Map Java method name and parameters to a JSON-RPC request (either map to set of Java Objects _or_ all the way to JSON)
051// (2) Map from received JSON-RPC response to JsonRpcResponse<RSLT> -- this response mapper function is configured as part of making the request.
052//
053// To abstract the specifics of Jackson from the (two) transport implementations. Basically methods/functions to
054// map from request to string/stream and to map from string/stream to response.  The java.net.http implementation has already defined
055// some functional interfaces for this, so coming up with an interface that both the java.net.http implementation and the HttpUrlConnection
056// implementation can use will lead to this "SECOND STEP"
057//
058// Now that JsonRpcClient is a generic with <T extends Type>, we have loosened the Jackson coupling somewhat.
059//
060/**
061 * A strongly-typed, Jackson-based JSON-RPC client. {@link JsonRpcClient} provides many convenience send `default` methods in a
062 * JSON-library-independent way. {@code DefaultRpcClient} adds support for Jackson. This class implements
063 * the constructors, static fields, and getters, but delegates the core
064 * {@link JsonRpcTransport#sendRequestForResponseAsync(JsonRpcRequest, Type)} method to a {@link JsonRpcTransport} implementation component.
065 * In constructors that don't take a {@link TransportFactory}, {@link JsonRpcClientJavaNet} will be used.
066 */
067public class DefaultRpcClient implements JsonRpcClient<JavaType> {
068    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DefaultRpcClient.class);
069    private static final JsonRpcMessage.Version DEFAULT_JSON_RPC_VERSION = JsonRpcMessage.Version.V2;
070
071    /**
072     * Functional interface for creating JsonRpcTransport instances from an {@link ObjectMapper}.
073     * <p>
074     * This is used to prevent a circular dependency on {@link ObjectMapper}.
075     */
076    @FunctionalInterface
077    public interface TransportFactory {
078        /**
079         * @param mapper mapper that is shared between {@code DefaultRpcClient} and the {@link JsonRpcTransport}.
080         * @return a transport instance
081         */
082        JsonRpcTransport<JavaType> create(ObjectMapper mapper);
083    }
084    protected final JsonRpcMessage.Version jsonRpcVersion;
085    protected final ObjectMapper mapper;
086    private final JavaType defaultType;
087    private final JsonRpcTransport<JavaType> transport;
088
089    public DefaultRpcClient(URI server, final String rpcUser, final String rpcPassword) {
090        this(JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), DEFAULT_JSON_RPC_VERSION, server, rpcUser, rpcPassword);
091    }
092
093    public DefaultRpcClient(JsonRpcMessage.Version jsonRpcVersion, URI server, final String rpcUser, final String rpcPassword) {
094        this(JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), jsonRpcVersion, server, rpcUser, rpcPassword);
095    }
096    public DefaultRpcClient(SSLContext sslContext, JsonRpcMessage.Version jsonRpcVersion, URI server, final String rpcUser, final String rpcPassword) {
097        this((m) -> new JsonRpcClientJavaNet(m, sslContext, server, rpcUser, rpcPassword), jsonRpcVersion);
098    }
099
100    public DefaultRpcClient(TransportFactory transportFactory, JsonRpcMessage.Version jsonRpcVersion) {
101        this.jsonRpcVersion = jsonRpcVersion;
102        mapper = new ObjectMapper();
103        // TODO: Provide external API to configure FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES
104        mapper.configure(DeserializationFeature.FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES, false);
105        defaultType = mapper.getTypeFactory().constructType(Object.class);
106        transport = transportFactory.create(mapper);
107    }
108
109    @Override
110    public JsonRpcMessage.Version getJsonRpcVersion() {
111        return jsonRpcVersion;
112    }
113
114    @Override
115    public URI getServerURI() {
116        return transport.getServerURI();
117    }
118
119    @Override
120    public <R> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<R>> sendRequestForResponseAsync(JsonRpcRequest request, JavaType responseType) {
121        return transport.sendRequestForResponseAsync(request, responseType);
122    }
123
124    /**
125     * Convenience method for requesting an asynchronous response with a {@link JsonNode} for the result.
126     * @param request The request to send
127     * @return A future JSON RPC Response with `result` of type {@code JsonNode}
128     */
129    public CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<JsonNode>> sendRequestForResponseAsync(JsonRpcRequest request) {
130        return sendRequestForResponseAsync(request, responseTypeFor(JsonNode.class));
131    }
132
133    public ObjectMapper getMapper() {
134        return mapper;
135    }
136
137    @Override
138    public JavaType defaultType() {
139        return defaultType;
140    }
141
142    @Override
143    public JavaType responseTypeFor(JavaType resultType) {
144        return getMapper().getTypeFactory().
145                constructParametricType(JsonRpcResponse.class, resultType);
146    }
147
148    @Override
149    public JavaType responseTypeFor(Class<?> resultType) {
150        return getMapper().getTypeFactory().
151                constructParametricType(JsonRpcResponse.class, resultType);
152    }
153
154    @Override
155    public JavaType typeForClass(Class<?> clazz) {
156        return getMapper().constructType(clazz);
157    }
158
159    @Override
160    public JavaType collectionTypeForClasses(Class<? extends Collection> collection, Class<?> clazz) {
161        return getMapper().getTypeFactory()
162                .constructCollectionType(collection, clazz);
163    }
164
165    @Override
166    public JavaType collectionTypeForClasses(Class<? extends Collection> collection, JavaType type) {
167        return getMapper().getTypeFactory()
168                .constructCollectionType(collection, type);
169    }
170
171    public <T> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<T>> pollOnce(JsonRpcRequest request, JavaType resultType, TransientErrorMapper<T> errorMapper) {
172        CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<T>> f = sendRequestForResponseAsync(request, responseTypeFor(resultType));
173        // In Java 12+ this can be replaced with exceptionallyCompose()
174        return f.handle((r, t) -> errorMapper.map(request, r, t))
175                .thenCompose(Function.identity());
176    }
177
178    /**
179     * A wait-for-server routine that is agnostic about which RPC methods the server supports. In addition to two {@link Duration}
180     * parameters, there are 2 lambda parameters to enable this method to work with any JSON-RPC server. This version will tell
181     * the JSON-RPC Mapper to return an {@link JsonRpcResponse} with a result of type {@link Object}. If you need more precise
182     * control over the type, use {@link #waitForServer(Duration, Duration, Supplier, JavaType, TransientErrorMapper)}.
183     * @param timeout how long to wait
184     * @param retry delay between retries
185     * @param requestSupplier supplier of requests (needs to increment request ID at the very least)
186     * @param errorMapper function that maps non-fatal errors (i.e. cases to keep polling)
187     * @return A future that returns a successful
188     * @param <T> The desired result type to be returned when the server is running
189     */
190    public <T> CompletableFuture<T> waitForServer(Duration timeout, Duration retry, Supplier<JsonRpcRequest> requestSupplier, TransientErrorMapper<T> errorMapper) {
191        return waitForServer(timeout,retry, requestSupplier, typeForClass(Object.class), errorMapper);
192    }
193
194    // TODO: What happens if you cancel this future?
195    /**
196    * A wait-for-server routine that is agnostic about which RPC methods the server supports. In addition to two {@link Duration}
197    * parameters, there are 3 parameters (2 functions and a generic type specifier) to enable this method to work with any JSON-RPC server.
198    * @param timeout how long to wait
199    * @param retry delay between retries
200    * @param requestSupplier supplier of requests (needs to increment request ID at the very least)
201    * @param resultType the result type for the response
202    * @param errorMapper function that maps non-fatal errors (i.e. cases to keep polling)
203    * @return A future that returns a successful
204    * @param <T> The desired result type to be returned when the server is running
205    */
206    public <T> CompletableFuture<T> waitForServer(Duration timeout, Duration retry, Supplier<JsonRpcRequest> requestSupplier, JavaType resultType, TransientErrorMapper<T> errorMapper) {
207        CompletableFuture<T> future = new CompletableFuture<>();
208        getDefaultAsyncExecutor().execute(() -> {
209            log.debug("Waiting for server RPC ready...");
210            String status;          // Status message for logging
211            String statusLast = null;
212            long seconds = 0;
213            while (seconds < timeout.toSeconds()) {
214                JsonRpcResponse<T> r;
215                try {
216                    // All non-fatal exceptions will be mapped to a JsonRpcError with code -20000
217                    r = this.pollOnce(requestSupplier.get(), resultType, errorMapper).get();
218                } catch (InterruptedException | ExecutionException e) {
219                    // If a fatal error occurred, fail our future and abort this thread
220                    log.error("Fatal exception: ", e);
221                    future.completeExceptionally(e);
222                    return;
223                }
224                if (r.getResult() != null) {
225                    // We received a response with a result, server is ready and has returned a usable result
226                    log.debug("RPC Ready.");
227                    future.complete(r.getResult());
228                    return;
229                }
230                // We received a response with a non-fatal error, log it and wait to retry.
231                status = statusFromErrorResponse(r);
232                // Log status messages only once, if new or updated
233                if (!status.equals(statusLast)) {
234                    log.info("Waiting for server: RPC Status: " + status);
235                    statusLast = status;
236                }
237                try {
238                    // Damnit, IntelliJ we're not busy-waiting we're polling!
239                    Thread.sleep(retry.toMillis());
240                    seconds += retry.toSeconds();
241                } catch (InterruptedException e) {
242                    log.error(e.toString());
243                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
244                    future.completeExceptionally(e);
245                    return;
246                }
247            }
248            String timeoutMessage = String.format("waitForServer() timed out after %d seconds", timeout.toSeconds());
249            log.error(timeoutMessage);
250            future.completeExceptionally(new TimeoutException(timeoutMessage));
251        });
252        return future;
253    }
254
255    /**
256     * Functional interface for ignoring what are considered "transient" errors. The definition of what is transient
257     * may vary depending upon the application. Different implementations of this function can be created for
258     * different applications.
259     * <p>
260     * The {@code JsonRpcResponse} returned may be a "synthetic" response, that is generated by the client,
261     * not by the server. The synthetic response will look like this:
262     *     <ul>
263     *         <li>error.code: -20000</li>
264     *         <li>error.message: "Server temporarily unavailable"</li>
265     *         <li>error.data: Detailed string message, e.g. "Connection refused"</li>
266     *     </ul>
267     * @param <T> The expected result type
268     */
269    @FunctionalInterface
270    public interface TransientErrorMapper<T> {
271        /**
272         * @param request The request we're handling completions for
273         * @param response response if one was successfully returned (or null)
274         * @param throwable exception if the call failed (or null)
275         * @return A completed or failed future than can replace the input (response, throwable) pair
276         */
277        CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<T>> map(JsonRpcRequest request, JsonRpcResponse<T> response, Throwable throwable);
278    }
279
280    /**
281     * Transient error mapper that is a no-op, i.e. it passes all errors through unchanged.
282     * @param <T> result type
283     * @return an unchanged completable future
284     */
285    protected  <T> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<T>> identityTransientErrorMapper(JsonRpcRequest request, JsonRpcResponse<T> response, Throwable t) {
286        return response != null
287                ? CompletableFuture.completedFuture(response)
288                : CompletableFuture.failedFuture(t);
289    }
290
291    protected <T> JsonRpcResponse<T> temporarilyUnavailableResponse(JsonRpcRequest request,  Throwable t) {
292        return new JsonRpcResponse<>(request, new JsonRpcError(-2000, "Server temporarily unavailable", t.getMessage()));
293    }
294
295    /**
296     * @param response A response where {@code getResult() == null}
297     * @return An error status string suitable for log messages
298     */
299    protected String statusFromErrorResponse(JsonRpcResponse<?> response) {
300        Objects.requireNonNull(response);
301        if (response.getResult() != null) {
302            throw new IllegalStateException("This should only be called for responses with null result");
303        }
304        if (response.getError() == null) {
305            return "Invalid response both result and error were null";
306        } else if (response.getError().getData() != null) {
307            // Has option data, possibly the -2000 special case
308            return response.getError().getData().toString();
309        } else {
310            return response.getError().getMessage();
311        }
312    }
313}
